name: Kettlebell Floor Press
resistance_profile: The floor stops the upper arm and removes the bottom stretch, keeping tension in the mid-range; the offset kettlebell mass adds a stability demand on the chest and shoulder.
cues: Keep the wrist stacked straight over the forearm
Touch the triceps to the floor, don't bounce
Drive each bell up and slightly back over the shoulder
execution_steps: Lie on your back on the floor with a kettlebell beside each shoulder, knees bent or legs straight.
Roll toward each bell, grip the handle, and bring the kettlebells to the racked position at the shoulders.
Press the kettlebells up over the chest until the arms are extended, letting each bell settle over the wrist.
Lower under control until the backs of the upper arms lightly touch the floor.
Pause briefly on the floor, then press back up; finish by safely lowering the bells to the floor.
good_when: You only have kettlebells or train at home
You want a shoulder-friendly press with a limited, pain-free range
Pressing without a bench or a spotter
bad_when: You want a full loaded chest stretch
You need small load increments — kettlebell jumps are large
You want maximal chest-building range of motion
common_errors: Bouncing the elbows off the floor
Letting the wrist bend back under the bell
Flaring the elbows out to 90 degrees
progressions: Progress to a dumbbell or barbell bench press for full range
regressions: Floor press with dumbbells for an easier grip
